expectations.text: States that resume state is rebuilt from GitHub issues/comments using the work item slug or machine data
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 keeps repo-local overrides, but Phases 1–11 persist durable state in GitHub issues and comments instead of local per-work-item markdown files.  `RESUME=2026-04-27-add-rate-limit-logs` rebuilds state from the GitHub issue hierarchy f

expectations.text: Does not instruct the agent to read `.coding-workflow/work/<slug>/`
Uses issue closure state and durable comments such as approvals or implementation log comments to determine what is complete
expectations.evidence: rtifact, and task issues, then read their bodies/comments as the machine-readable record. Do not rely on `.coding-workflow/work/<slug>/`.  The next phase is whichever phase is still incomplete according to GitHub state: closed phase issues

expectations.text: Makes clear that GitHub is the canonical durable record for Phases 1–11
expectations.evidence: `, so an agent with no local artifact directory can resume by inspecting closed phase issues, open task/artifact issues, task `stage`/dependency metadata, and gate conditions from GitHub alone.

eval_feedback.overall: Deterministic text grading against the eval expectations.
